description_long: This is a medium-sized, orange plastic bucket with a simple, tapered cylindrical shape and a wide, slightly flared rim. The bucket features a single gray bail handle, likely made of metal or plastic, attached near the rim on opposite sides for easy carrying. The surface is smooth and unadorned, with no visible markings, labels, or decorative elements. The interior appears hollow, suitable for holding liquids or loose materials, and the rim is rounded for comfort and structural integrity. There are no visible reinforcements, bands, or additional structural features on the body. The bucket is utilitarian in design, commonly found in urban environments for cleaning, carrying water, or general utility tasks.
description: A smooth, orange plastic bucket with a gray bail handle and a wide, flared rim. It is unmarked and utilitarian, suitable for carrying liquids or materials.
description_view_0: Front view showing the plain orange bucket with no visible handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the gray bail handle attached to the rim on the right side.
description_view_2: Rear view with the gray bail handle fully visible, arching over the bucket's opening.
description_view_3: Side view with the gray bail handle attached to the rim on the left side.
